title: Heatwave in Udhampur as North India prepares for weather shift - The News Mill
full_text:
Udhampur in Jammu & Kashmir continues to experience intense heatwave conditions, which the India Meteorological Department (IMD) expects to last until January 31, 2026. While severe heat affects Udhampur currently, the IMD has issued a forecast indicating changing weather patterns across North India.
Maximum temperatures across the region are predicted to increase by 2-4°C until January 31, 2026, followed by a gradual decrease of 3-5°C. A heatwave warning remains effective for isolated areas in Jammu-Kashmir-Ladakh-Gilgit-Baltistan-Muzaffarabad, Himachal Pradesh, Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi, West Rajasthan, and Uttar Pradesh through January 31.
A significant weather shift is expected as a fresh Western Disturbance influences the Western Himalayan region starting January 31. This system will prompt a transition across much of North India.
The IMD forecasts isolated to scattered rainfall from January 31 to February 5 in Jammu & Kashmir, Himachal Pradesh, and Uttarakhand. Rainfall is also likely to extend to Punjab, Haryana, Delhi, and parts of Uttar Pradesh and Rajasthan between February 1 and 5.
This atmospheric change is accompanied by warnings for thunder squalls with wind speeds reaching 50-60 km/h, gusty winds, and lightning over Rajasthan, Haryana, Delhi, and Uttar Pradesh from February 1 to 2. Isolated hailstorm activity is anticipated in Himachal Pradesh, Jammu & Kashmir, and Uttarakhand during February 1-2, with similar hazards predicted in Haryana, Chandigarh, and Delhi on February 2.
Residents are advised to monitor local weather updates closely and prepare to move to safer locations if conditions deteriorate.

title: Forest fires in Rajouri, Jammu and Kashmir amid heatwave - The News Mill
full_text:
Massive forest fires broke out on Wednesday across various locations in Rajouri district, Jammu and Kashmir, officials reported. The fires have spread through several forest ranges, with the prevailing heatwave and prolonged dry spell being attributed as the main causes.
Personnel from the Forest Department alongside local authorities are actively engaged in efforts to extinguish the flames. Further details are yet to be released.
The recent incidents follow reports from a week earlier about multiple fire outbreaks in the district. On June 3, Sat Pal, Conservator of Forests (West Circle), Rajouri, stated that approximately 45 forest fire incidents had been recorded in the preceding 12 weeks across the Rajouri and Nowshera forest divisions.
The Sialsui Khadar forest area within Kalakote tehsil of the Rajouri Forest Division experienced significant damage due to the hot and dry weather conditions.
Speaking to ANI, Sat Pal said, “During the current forest fire season, we have witnessed a significant number of fire incidents, specifically over the last 12 weeks. In Rajouri district, the total number of incidents recorded stands at approximately 45. This figure encompasses both the Rajouri and Nowshera divisions. After some rainfall yesterday, temperatures have dropped slightly. We are therefore hopeful that the frequency of forest fire incidents will decrease in the coming days.”
He noted that a general meeting was convened to discuss rapid resource mobilisation and improved communication mechanisms aimed at minimising the initial spread of forest fires. “A general meeting was held to discuss rapid resource mobilisation and improved communication to minimise the initial spread of fires. Although these are surface fires and not crown fires, they still cause significant damage to biomass, birds, animals, reptiles and other wildlife,” he said.
Sat Pal urged the public to cooperate in preventing forest fires, appealing to residents to avoid negligence and take necessary precautions. “I appeal to the public to cooperate immediately in areas affected by forest fires, avoid negligence and take precautions to prevent such incidents. It is important that we protect our collective green assets,” he added.
Earlier, multiple forest fire incidents were reported in the Rajouri Forest Division amid the ongoing heatwave. Teams from the Forest Department, Forest Protection Force, Social Forestry Department and local volunteers have been deployed to firefight and contain the fires to prevent them from spreading to denser forest areas.

title: जम्मू-कश्मीर में भीषण गर्मी के बीच मौसम विभाग ने जारी किया बड़ा अलर्ट, अगले 5 दिन बरसेंगे ओले और जमकर होगी बारिश! - Zee News
search_term: ("हीट एक्शन प्लान" OR "गर्मी से राहत" OR चेतावनी OR "रेड अलर्ट" OR "ऑरेंज अलर्ट" OR "येलो अलर्ट" OR एडवाइजरी OR "राहत सामग्री" OR "राहत शिविर" OR मुआवजा) Jammu and Kashmir
full_text:
Jammu: बीते कुछ दिनों से जम्मू-कश्मीर के लोग आसमान से बरसती आग और चिलचिलाती धूप से बेहाल हैं. बुधवार को भी सुबह से ही तेज धूप ने लोगों के पसीने छुड़ा दिए. दोपहर होते-होते हीटवेव (लू) के थपेड़ों ने रफ्तार पकड़ी, जिससे सड़कें और बाजार सूने नजर आए. लोग छांव और ठंडे पानी की तलाश में भटकते दिखे. लेकिन, इसी बीच मौसम विभाग (IMD) ने एक ऐसी खबर दी है, जो राहत के साथ-साथ आफत भी ला सकती है.
आज शाम से बदलेगा मौसम का मिजाज
मौसम विभाग के मुताबिक, आज यानी 17 जून की देर शाम से ही मौसम करवट लेने वाला है. दोपहर बाद से ही कई इलाकों में बादल छाने, हल्की बारिश होने और गरज-चमक के साथ तेज हवाएं चलने की पूरी संभावना है.
18 से 22 जून: अगले 5 दिन रहें सावधान!
असली खेल 18 जून से शुरू होने वाला है. मौसम विभाग ने चेतावनी जारी की है कि 18 से 22 जून के बीच जम्मू-कश्मीर में तेज बौछारें, ओलावृष्टि (Hailstorm), बिजली कड़कने और आंधी चलने के आसार हैं. इसके बाद 23 से 25 जून तक भी आसमान में बादल छाए रहेंगे और कुछ जगहों पर हल्की-फुल्की बारिश का दौर जारी रहेगा.
फ्लैश फ्लड और मडस्लाइड का खतरा, IMD ने जारी की एडवाइजरी
राहत की इस बारिश के बीच मौसम विभाग ने एक गंभीर चेतावनी भी दी है. अगले 5 दिनों में कुछ जगहों पर भारी ओलावृष्टि और तेज बारिश के कारण फ्लैश फ्लड (अचानक बाढ़) और मडस्लाइड (मिट्टी धंसने) का खतरा मंडरा रहा है.
प्रशासन ने लोगों के लिए ये जरूरी गाइडलाइंस जारी की हैं:
मौसम का यह यू-टर्न गर्मी से राहत तो देगा, लेकिन पहाड़ों पर यात्रा करने वालों को इस दौरान खास सावधानी बरतने की जरूरत है.
Source: Punjab Kesari
